summary: Russia has established at least five new drone launch sites near the Belarus border, increasing pressure on Ukrainian air defenses. Ukrainian forces report heightened threats of Oreshnik missiles and medium‑range ballistic missiles from the Kapustin Yar training ground. Multiple UAV groups are observed moving toward frontline cities such as Kryvyi Rih, Pavlohrad, and Kharkiv, indicating intensified aerial activity.
key_points: Russia created five new drone launch complexes near the Belarusian border in Bryansk, Oryol and Smolensk regions, complicating Ukrainian air defence (Source: @UkraineNow/81876).
Ukrainian air forces warn of a high probability of Oreshnik missile launches from Kapustin Yar within the next day (Source: @UkraineNow/81865).
A similar alert was issued for a medium‑range ballistic missile from Kapustin Yar (Source: @kpszsu/64925).
UAV groups were reported heading toward Kryvyi Rih from the south (Source: @kpszsu/64934).
UAVs were observed moving eastward toward Pavlohrad from the east (Source: @kpszsu/64935).
Tactical Russian aviation activity increased over southeastern and eastern sectors, raising the threat of air‑to‑ground strikes (Source: @kpszsu/64908).
UAVs from the Black Sea area were directed toward the Odesa region (Source: @kpszsu/64929).

sections.executive_summary: Russia has opened at least five new drone launch sites close to the Belarus border, enhancing its ability to conduct massed UAV attacks. Ukrainian air command issued alerts for imminent Oreshnik missile and medium‑range ballistic missile launches from Kapustin Yar. Concurrently, numerous UAV groups are moving toward key Ukrainian cities, indicating a surge in aerial pressure.

points: The Ukrainian Ministry of Defence announced reforms to service terms and pay, aiming to sustain military readiness (Source: @UkraineNow/81862).
President Zelensky stated that rear‑area troops will receive a minimum of 30,000 UAH monthly, while frontline soldiers will average 300,000 UAH (Source: @UkraineNow/81874).
Ukraine seeks an additional $20 billion from allies to maintain its battlefield advantage (Source: @UkraineNow/81860).
Ukrainian forces reported successful strikes on two Russian oil‑chemical plants (Source: @UkraineNow/81886).
Russia established at least five new drone launch sites near the Belarusian border in Bryansk, Oryol and Smolensk regions, with the largest near the village of Tsymbulovo (Source: @UkraineNow/81876).
Russian tactical aviation increased activity over southeastern and eastern directions, threatening frontline oblasts (Source: @kpszsu/64908).
Russian forces are preparing to use Oreshnik missiles from Kapustin Yar, with high probability of launch within 24 hours (Source: @UkraineNow/81865).
A medium‑range ballistic missile launch from Kapustin Yar is also deemed highly probable (Source: @kpszsu/64925).
UAV groups were observed heading toward Kryvyi Rih from the south (Source: @kpszsu/64934).
UAVs moved eastward toward Pavlohrad from the east (Source: @kpszsu/64935).
UAVs from the Black Sea area were directed toward the Odesa region (Source: @kpszsu/64929).
UAV activity reported over Sumy heading toward Konotop (Source: @kpszsu/64933).
UAVs from northern Chernihiv region flew along the border with Belarus toward northern Kyiv region (Source: @kpszsu/64948).
UAVs were sighted over Zhytomyr heading toward Korosten (Source: @kpszsu/64957).
UAVs observed over Chernihiv along the Dnipro corridor toward Dobryanka (Source: @eRadarrua/96348).
UAVs reported over Sumy moving toward Konotop (Source: @kpszsu/64933).
UAVs were reported heading toward Kharkiv from the north (Source: @kpszsu/64936).
UAVs observed over Dnipropetrovsk moving toward Pavlohrad from the east (Source: @kpszsu/64937).
UAVs from the Black Sea area were directed toward the Odesa region (Source: @kpszsu/64929).
UAV activity noted over Poltava region from Karlivka toward Poltava (Source: @eRadarrua/96350).
Ukrainian air forces issued alerts for possible Oreshnik missile launches from Kapustin Yar within the next day (Source: @UkraineNow/81865).
A medium‑range ballistic missile launch from Kapustin Yar was also flagged as highly probable (Source: @kpszsu/64925).
Russian tactical aviation increased sorties over southeastern and eastern fronts, raising the risk of air‑to‑ground attacks (Source: @kpszsu/64908).

UAV groups from multiple directions (south, east, north) are targeting key Ukrainian cities, indicating a coordinated aerial campaign (Sources: @kpszsu/64934, @kpszsu/64935, @kpszsu/64957, @kpszsu/64948).

key_developments: Russia has deployed at least five new drone launch sites within tens of kilometres of the Belarusian border, enhancing its capacity for massed UAV strikes (Source: @UkraineNow/81876).
Ukrainian air command warned of a high likelihood of Oreshnik missile launches from Kapustin Yar in the next 24 hours (Source: @UkraineNow/81865).
A medium‑range ballistic missile launch from Kapustin Yar is also considered imminent (Source: @kpszsu/64925).
UAV formations are moving toward Kryvyi Rih from the south, increasing pressure on central‑southern defenses (Source: @kpszsu/64934).
UAVs heading east toward Pavlohrad suggest intensified targeting of the Dnipropetrovsk axis (Source: @kpszsu/64935).
Russian tactical aviation activity has risen over southeastern and eastern sectors, heightening the threat of air‑to‑ground attacks (Source: @kpszsu/64908).
UAVs from the Black Sea are directed toward the Odesa region, indicating a focus on southern coastal areas (Source: @kpszsu/64929).
sections.threat_assessment: The establishment of new Russian drone launch complexes near Belarus markedly expands Moscow's ability to conduct sustained UAV operations against Ukrainian rear areas. Simultaneous alerts for Oreshnik and medium‑range ballistic missiles from Kapustin Yar suggest a coordinated escalation of both missile and drone threats. Ukrainian air defence will face compounded pressure from multiple UAV vectors converging on key logistical hubs, demanding rapid adaptation of interception assets.
sections.regional_response: Ukrainian command is likely to prioritize reinforcement of air defence systems along the northern, eastern, and southern corridors where UAV influxes are reported. Coordination with allied air‑defence providers may increase to counter the anticipated missile launches from Kapustin Yar. Mobilisation of additional surface‑to‑air missile batteries to protect critical infrastructure, especially near Kryvyi Rih and Odesa, is expected.
sections.intelligence_notes: Continued satellite monitoring of the newly identified drone sites near the Belarus border is essential to track operational readiness. Signals intelligence should focus on launch preparations at Kapustin Yar to provide early warning of missile launches. UAV flight path analysis will help predict future target selections and allocate interception resources efficiently.
